name: Don't Repeat Yourself (DRY)
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 3: Functions
problem: The same algorithm, logic, or data appears in multiple places in the codebase. When requirements change, every duplicate must be found and updated consistently, and each copy is a potential source of divergent bugs.
steps: Identify duplicated code segments that implement the same algorithm or rule.
Extract the common logic into a single named function, method, or class.
Replace all duplicates with calls to the extracted abstraction.
Apply the Rule of Three: refactor once the same logic appears for the third time.
Verify after extraction that all prior call sites produce the same behavior as before.
